mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in nonfarm payrolls signals expanding labor income and consumer spending capacity, which drives aggregate earnings expectations higher and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are not positive in more than 50% of months with rising PAYEMS, or the Rank IC between PAYEMS changes and 20-day returns is below 0.03 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8%, elevated labor slack suppresses wage growth and consumer spending, which drags on corporate revenue expectations and causes negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Average 60-day forward returns are non-negative when UNRATE exceeds 8% over a 3-year rolling window, or the strategy Sharpe ratio is below 0.2 during triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous occurrence of accommodative monetary policy, proxied by a low federal funds rate, and improving labor markets, proxied by rising nonfarm payrolls, jointly lower discount rates and boost future cash flow expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with zero interest rate policy
failure_condition: The interaction term between FEDFUNDS levels and PAYEMS changes does not significantly predict 20-day returns (t-stat < 2.0) in rolling 2-year regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth accelerates corporate revenue only when monetary policy is highly accommodative; when the federal funds rate is below 1%, month-over-month increases in retail sales translate into positive earnings surprises that drive S&P 500 gains over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: RSAFS increases fail to produce positive 5-day returns in more than 50% of months when FEDFUNDS is below 1%.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in industrial production signals recovering manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates into higher operating leverage and earnings growth, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month changes in INDPRO do not coincide with positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in at least 55% of observations over a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer prices combined with a near-zero federal funds rate squeeze real corporate margins because input costs increase without offsetting monetary easing, and the resulting profit compression drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
regime_hint: zero lower bound with inflation uncertainty
failure_condition: The joint condition of rising CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS below 0.5% is followed by positive 60-day returns in more than 45% of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in housing starts reflect strengthening residential investment and construction employment, which raises demand for building materials, durable goods, and credit, lifting broad corporate profits and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month HOUST increases are not followed by positive 60-day returns in at least 50% of instances over any 3-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production remains below 95, the economy is operating with significant idle capacity; month-over-month increases in output from this depressed level signal a sharp earnings rebound for cyclical sectors, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO levels below 95 combined with monthly increases produce negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of triggered observations.
